summary: R2 revision replaces the React/SVG per-entity visible map with a MapRenderer-owned Pixi-mounted surface in the web shell. React now owns only UI intent and operational DOM state: mode, zoom, selected entity, filters, sorting, list scroll, and details panel state. The browser-visible map receives read-model replacement through MapRenderer applyDelta/rebuild semantics with viewport mode, zoom, and selected entity. Map presentation instructions for colors, route width, labels, and selection visuals remain in packages/map-renderer.

tests_run.result: Architecture boundaries passed; self-test fixture produced 15 expected violations.

decisions: Moved visible map entity rendering out of packages/ui/src/index.tsx; UI now receives a mapSurface ReactElement slot and renders only controls, list, and details DOM.

Moved Pixi mounting into apps/web/src/shell/web-client-shell.tsx using MapRenderer mount/applyDelta ownership; React passes read-model snapshot, viewport, and selection callback but does not create per-map-entity display objects.
Kept map presentation formulas in packages/map-renderer: district fill, seasonal palette, route stroke/width, label generation, and selection styling.
Kept sim-core, save/schema, Electron IPC, RNG/time, and production dependency scope unchanged.
risks: The M2 web map still uses the protocol-shaped client fixture pending live worker query integration, matching the existing non-blocking M2 risk.
Focused gates passed for R2; broader build/typecheck/e2e should be rerun by reviewer/integration if required before final acceptance.
requested_action: review the R2 Pixi/MapRenderer-owned visible map fix.
